talk_metadata.talk_title: Advocacy and the Emerging KRAS Landscape in Pancreatic Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Revolution Medicines, who was doing the first ever Phase III trial for pancreatic cancer patients with a drug targeting KRAS, announced positive results"

key_data_gaps: No specific efficacy metrics or survival data presented
No safety or toxicity data disclosed

crispro_opportunity.description: Speaker highlighted KRAS-targeted therapy but did not discuss biomarker selection or patient stratification, indicating a gap for computational biomarker platforms.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Revolution Medicines... announced positive results, and there is a lot of activity in the KRAS space"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop predictive models to identify patients most likely to benefit from KRAS inhibitors based on genomic and transcriptomic profiles.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: Identify the specific Revolution Medicines KRAS Phase III trial (NCT number)
PubMed: Review published efficacy and safety data for the KRAS inhibitor

cited_competitors.name: Revolution Medicines
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as conducting a Phase III KRAS-targeted trial with positive results

unresolved_questions: What were the specific efficacy outcomes (e.g., OS, PFS) of the KRAS-targeted Phase III trial?
What safety or toxicity profile was observed in that trial?
